title: festuca scirpifolia subsp. tarmensis
intro: Festuca scirpifolia subsp. tarmensis, also known as Tarm fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the Andes mountain range in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Festuca scirpifolia subsp. tarmensis can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the plant's active compounds with a solvent.
